context: Checkpoint 2026-04-29 preparing context compaction after advancing host-proxied streaming in small atomic slices. Main lane: pi-agent streaming AgentResponse. We deliberately chose long-term extensibility/security over rushing direct wasi::http. Provider credentials and route policy stay in Tractor host. Real streaming remains disabled until host-proxied stream implementation is proven.
current_tasks: Unpushed commits since origin/develop include: 2b91ffe1 fix(agent): keep streaming sink model current; a03d038a docs(agent): note fallback stream sink model; fef70936 docs(agent): clarify streaming transport boundary; 9d801190 docs(agent): propose host-proxied llm streaming; 124adfef test(agent): cover stream sink state; b28a8f65 docs(agent): sketch host streaming contract; a388716e docs(agent): link streaming transport adr; 36b88e37 feat(tractor): add llm stream bridge contract; 3797f13c docs(agent): mark stream bridge contract; 29342d57 chore(project): refresh stream bridge handoff; 987318e4 test(tractor): cover stream bridge placeholder; 6eb7742c refactor(agent): expose host stream bridge seam; 8f74d439 docs(agent): note host stream wrapper seam.

ADR-053 exists at specs/ADRs/ADR-053-host-proxied-llm-streaming.md. It proposes host-proxied LLM streaming and rejects direct plugin wasi::http until a later ADR accepts credential/security trade-offs.

WIT contract added to packages/tractor/wit/host/refarm-plugin-host.wit. Note: packages/pi-agent/wit/refarm-plugin-host.wit is a tracked symlink-like file containing ../../tractor/wit/host/refarm-plugin-host.wit, so edits happen in Tractor WIT. Contract adds stream-response-metadata, stream-response-result, and complete-http-stream(..., stream-metadata).

Tractor implementation in packages/tractor/src/host/wasi_bridge/core.rs implements complete_http_stream as buffered compatibility placeholder: calls existing llm_complete_http, returns final_body, preserves last_sequence, stored_chunks=0. Helper buffered_stream_response_result is unit-tested.

New Tractor tests: packages/tractor/src/host/wasi_bridge_tests/llm_stream_contract.rs, included by wasi_bridge_tests.rs, assert buffered placeholder preserves final_body/last_sequence and never claims stored chunks.

pi-agent provider.rs now has HostStreamRequestMetadata, HostStreamResponse, and http_post_stream_via_host wrapper seam around generated llm_bridge::complete_http_stream. It is intentionally dead-code for now until streaming_reader_available can flip with real host implementation.

pi-agent stream sink state was refactored/tested: StreamResponseSinkState::new/update_model/update_last_sequence preserves sequence across fallback model updates and ignores None sequence updates. Tests in packages/pi-agent/src/tests/streaming_sink_tests.rs.
ROADMAP streaming section updated to mark host-proxied WIT contract and pi-agent wrapper seam done, while real chunked HTTP/SSE through complete-http-stream remains pending.
blockers: Real streaming is still disabled. streaming_reader_available() remains false and provider_stream_request_enabled_from_env() remains false even with LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES=1.
complete-http-stream is a placeholder only; it is not live streaming and stored_chunks=0 by design.
Disk dropped back to ~8.0GB free after local Rust checks despite clean:light. Rust targets remain ~4.85GB. Avoid broad builds; consider clean:heavy before expensive checks if disk falls further.
GitHub Actions has not been run for the new unpushed stream-bridge stack. Local reproduction/checks passed; if/when pushing, use gh run watch --exit-status, not polling.
next_actions: Before more work after compaction: git status --short; git log --oneline origin/develop..HEAD; df -h /workspaces/refarm; npm run clean:rust:check --silent.
Next technical slice: implement or design tests for complete-http-stream live internals with a fake SSE server. Do not flip streaming_reader_available until end-to-end partial persistence is proven.

Decide implementation shape: host stores AgentResponse chunks directly, host stores generic StreamChunk nodes, or a safer resource/callback stream. Favor generic/extensible host boundary; avoid duplicating provider parser if possible.

If pushing current stack: local checks already run: cd packages/pi-agent && cargo check --target wasm32-wasip1 --quiet; cd packages/tractor && cargo check --quiet; cargo test --lib buffered_stream_response_result --quiet; npm run project:validate --silent; git diff --check. Then use git push origin develop and gh run watch <run-id> --repo aretw0/refarm --exit-status.
Potential cleanup before push/checks: npm run clean:light --silent already run. If disk <8GB and more Rust checks needed, consider npm run clean:heavy --silent, accepting rebuild cost.
open_questions: Should live complete-http-stream store AgentResponse nodes directly (satisfies current UI contract but couples Tractor to pi-agent schema) or generic StreamChunk nodes (more extensible but needs projection/reassembly)?
Can Component Model safely support host-to-guest callbacks during imported complete_http_stream, or should we avoid reentrancy? Current ADR prefers host-owned append-only stream record.
Should provider-specific SSE parsing be extracted to a shared Rust crate used by both pi-agent and Tractor?
How should partial chunks record tokens/duration before final provider usage arrives? Currently partials use zero tokens/duration; final carries usage.
Error/cancel semantics after partial chunks are still undefined.
key_decisions_pending: AgentResponse direct chunks vs generic StreamChunk node.
Shared SSE parser extraction vs host duplication.
When to push the current unpushed stack.
